model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_performan.name: stg_performance
model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_performan.description:
Staging view over raw_performance (Lattice). One row per (employee,
review_cycle). 27 standard cycles span 2020-H2 through 2024-H2.
Eligible employees are those with at least 90 days tenure by the
cycle's completion date (Jul 15 / Jan 15) and not yet terminated.
Calculated fields (rating_numeric, is_top_performer) derive in
intermediate / marts.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description:
FK to stg_employees. Not every employee has a review row --
2025 Q1 hires and short-tenured Early Churners may have no
rows because no eligible cycle has elapsed yet.

review_cycle.name: review_cycle
review_cycle.description:
Identifier for the review period. Format YYYY-HN (e.g.
'2024-H2'). H1 covers Jan-Jun completed Jul 15; H2 covers
Jul-Dec completed Jan 15 of the following year.

review_completed_date.name: review_completed_date
review_completed_date.description:
The date the review cycle was finalized. Always within +/-7
days of the canonical cycle date (Jul 15 / Jan 15) per
Section 12 HR13.

review_status.name: review_status
review_status.description:
Whether the review was completed for this employee. Spec
target distribution: 95% Completed / 4% Incomplete / 1%
Exempt. Exempt is reserved for M5 leadership reviews.

overall_rating.name: overall_rating
overall_rating.description:
Final calibrated performance rating for the cycle. Mapped to
rating_numeric (SE=5, E=4, M=3, PM=2, DNM=1) in downstream
models for quantitative analysis.

manager_rating.name: manager_rating
manager_rating.description:
Direct manager's assessment before calibration. Same 5-point
scale as overall_rating. Matches overall_rating ~90% of the
time; occasionally drifts +/- 1 level reflecting calibration
adjustments.

self_rating.name: self_rating
self_rating.description:
The employee's self-assessment submitted during the review
cycle. Same 5-point scale. Nullable per spec (not every
employee submits a self assessment); always populated in this
dataset. ~30% of self ratings are one level higher than the
calibrated overall rating, reflecting typical optimism bias.

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_performan.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        stg_performance
    Layer:        staging
    Source:       seeds/raw_performance.csv  (Lattice - Performance Reviews)
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml staging defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Type-cast and lightly clean the seeded raw_performance CSV. One row
    per (employee, review_cycle). Output is a typed 1:1 view of the
    source rows; calculated fields (rating_numeric, is_top_performer)
    derive in intermediate / marts where they can be reused.

    Source dependencies
    -------------------
    - seeds/raw_performance.csv (loaded into raw.raw_performance by dbt seed)

    Business rules applied
    ----------------------
    - All STRING / DATE columns pass through unchanged. self_rating is
      nullable per spec (not every employee submits a self assessment);
      every other column is non-null in this dataset.
    - The H1 / H2 cycle schedule (Jul 15 / Jan 15 +/- 7 days) and the
      hire-date + 90-day eligibility rule are validated upstream by
      Section 12 HR11 and HR13.
    - Promotion coherence (employee promoted at date X must show Exceeds
      or higher in the cycle ending immediately before X) is validated
      upstream by HR14. The post-process upgrade pass in
      10_build_raw_performance.py guarantees zero violations on the
      seeded data.

    Calculated fields routed downstream
    -----------------------------------
        rating_numeric    SE=5, E=4, M=3, PM=2, DNM=1 (per data dict)
        is_top_performer  TRUE when overall_rating IN ('Significantly
                          Exceeds', 'Exceeds') for 2 consecutive cycles
        cycle_end_date    parsed from review_cycle (e.g. '2024-H2' ->
                          2025-01-15) for join logic
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with source as (
    select * from {{ ref('raw_performance') }}
),

renamed as (
    select
        -- Foreign key to stg_employees
        employee_id,

        -- Cycle identity
        review_cycle,
        review_completed_date,
        review_status,

        -- Ratings (same 5-point scale across all three)
        overall_rating,
        manager_rating,
        self_rating
    from source
)

select * from renamed

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_job_histo.name: stg_job_history
model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_job_histo.description:
Staging view over raw_job_history (ADP). One row per career event
per employee. Old/new fields capture the state on either side of
the change; old_* are null on Hire rows. Calculated fields
(months_since_last_promotion, total_promotions,
has_manager_change_last_12mo, career_velocity) derive in
intermediate / marts.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description:
FK to stg_employees. Every employee has at least one row
(the Hire event); active employees typically have 1-5 rows.

effective_date.name: effective_date
effective_date.description:
The date the change officially took effect in the HRIS.
Strictly between the employee's hire_date and termination_date
(or current date for active employees) per Section 12 HR4.

change_type.name: change_type
change_type.description:
Classification of the event. "Title Change" rows in this
dataset specifically encode the Manager Step-Back archetype's
M1 -> IC4 demotion (no native "Step-back" change_type exists
in the spec).

old_job_level.name: old_job_level
old_job_level.description:
Job level immediately before this change. Null for Hire events
(no prior state).

new_job_level.name: new_job_level
new_job_level.description:
Job level after this change. For Hire events, the starting
level. Always populated.

old_department.name: old_department
old_department.description: Department before this change. Null for Hire events.

new_department.name: new_department
new_department.description:
Department after this change. Always populated.
No Department Transfer rows exist in this dataset (only Lateral
Transfer within a department, plus the manager / level events).

old_sub_department.name: old_sub_department
old_sub_department.description: Sub-department before this change. Null for Hire events.

new_sub_department.name: new_sub_department
new_sub_department.description:
Sub-department after this change. Null only on Hire / event
rows for the CEO (Executive department has no sub-department).

old_job_title.name: old_job_title
old_job_title.description: Job title before this change. Null for Hire events.

new_job_title.name: new_job_title
new_job_title.description: Job title after this change. Always populated.

old_manager_id.name: old_manager_id
old_manager_id.description:
Manager_id before this change. Null for Hire events.
Self-FK to stg_employees.employee_id.

new_manager_id.name: new_manager_id
new_manager_id.description:
Manager_id after this change. Null only on Maya Chen's Hire
row (CEO reports to the Board, not an employee).
Self-FK to stg_employees.employee_id.

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_job_histo.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        stg_job_history
    Layer:        staging
    Source:       seeds/raw_job_history.csv  (ADP - Job Change History)
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml staging defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Type-cast and lightly clean the seeded raw_job_history CSV. One row per
    career event per employee, ordered chronologically. Output is a typed
    1:1 view of the source rows; no calculated time-to-promotion,
    career_velocity, total_promotions, or has_manager_change_last_12mo
    fields are introduced here -- those derive in intermediate / marts.

    Source dependencies
    -------------------
    - seeds/raw_job_history.csv (loaded into raw.raw_job_history by dbt seed)

    Business rules applied
    ----------------------
    - All old_* fields (old_job_level / old_department / old_sub_department
      / old_job_title / old_manager_id) are null on Hire rows -- the Hire
      event has no prior state.
    - Title Change rows in this dataset specifically encode the Manager
      Step-Back archetype's M1 -> IC4 demotion. The data dictionary's
      change_type domain has no native "Step-back" / "Demotion" value, so
      Title Change is the closest fit.
    - Sequential level progression on Promotion rows (one IC step at a
      time, IC -> M jumps directly to M1, M -> M one step at a time) is
      validated upstream by HR5 in Section 12 coherence.
    - Section 12 also enforces no events after termination_date (HR4),
      most-recent-event matches stg_employees current state (HR3), and
      Hire row's effective_date equals stg_employees.hire_date (HR1).
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with source as (
    select * from {{ ref('raw_job_history') }}
),

renamed as (
    select
        -- Foreign key to stg_employees
        employee_id,

        -- Event identity
        effective_date,
        change_type,

        -- Level transition
        old_job_level,
        new_job_level,

        -- Department / sub-department transition
        old_department,
        new_department,
        old_sub_department,
        new_sub_department,

        -- Title transition
        old_job_title,
        new_job_title,

        -- Manager transition
        old_manager_id,
        new_manager_id
    from source
)

select * from renamed

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_compensat.name: stg_compensation
model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_compensat.description:
Staging view over raw_compensation (Pave). One row per compensation
event per employee. Calculated compa_ratio (salary / comp_band_mid)
and band_position ((salary - comp_band_min) / (comp_band_max -
comp_band_min)) derive in intermediate / marts where they can be
reused across consumers. Section 12 invariants (salary within band,
one New Hire row per employee at hire_date, no records after
termination, Promotion rows match raw_job_history on the same date)
are validated upstream.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description:
FK to stg_employees. Every employee has at least one comp row
(the New Hire record at hire_date).

effective_date.name: effective_date
effective_date.description:
The date this compensation record became effective. Always
between the employee's hire_date (inclusive) and termination
date (or current date for active employees) per Section 12 HR8.

change_reason.name: change_reason
change_reason.description:
The reason for the comp change. Equity Adjustment is part of
the spec's domain but is not generated in this dataset.

salary.name: salary
salary.description:
Annual base salary in USD. Excludes equity, bonus, and other
variable compensation. Always within [comp_band_min,
comp_band_max] for the cell (Section 12 HR7).

comp_band_min.name: comp_band_min
comp_band_min.description:
Minimum of the function-specific compensation band for the
employee's (department, sub_department, job_level) at this
effective_date. Adjusted for the year of the record per the
spec's historical band multipliers (2020 = 0.85x, 2021 = 0.88x,
2022 = 0.92x, 2023 = 0.95x, 2024 = 0.98x, 2025 = 1.00x).

comp_band_mid.name: comp_band_mid
comp_band_mid.description:
Midpoint of the comp band. Used as the denominator for
compa_ratio in downstream models (compa_ratio = salary /
comp_band_mid).

comp_band_max.name: comp_band_max
comp_band_max.description:
Maximum of the comp band. Salary is clipped to this ceiling
when bumps would otherwise exceed it.

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_compensat.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        stg_compensation
    Layer:        staging
    Source:       seeds/raw_compensation.csv  (Pave - Compensation Data)
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml staging defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Type-cast and lightly clean the seeded raw_compensation CSV. One row
    per comp event per employee. Output is a typed 1:1 view of the source
    rows; calculated compa_ratio and band_position derive in intermediate
    / marts where they can be reused across consumers.

    Source dependencies
    -------------------
    - seeds/raw_compensation.csv (loaded into raw.raw_compensation by dbt seed)

    Business rules applied
    ----------------------
    - All four salary columns (salary, comp_band_min/mid/max) are FLOAT64
      per spec. The Python pipeline rounds salary to the nearest $500 but
      the type stays FLOAT to match the data dictionary.
    - Section 12 HR7 enforces salary in [comp_band_min, comp_band_max]
      upstream; HR6 enforces a New Hire row per employee with
      effective_date = stg_employees.hire_date; HR8 enforces no comp
      records after termination_date; HR9 enforces every Promotion row
      in raw_job_history has a matching Pave row on the same date; HR10
      enforces no orphan comp record on Lateral Transfer / Manager
      Change events. None of those are re-checked here -- staging trusts
      the loaded shape.

    Per-event semantics
    -------------------
    change_reason values present in this dataset:
        New Hire           one per employee, effective_date = hire_date
        Promotion          matches every Promotion row in raw_job_history
        Annual Review      Jan 15 each year of tenure (skips < 6mo and
                            Promotion years)
        Market Adjustment  2023-09-01 for every employee active that day
        Equity Adjustment  reserved by spec; not generated in this dataset
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with source as (
    select * from {{ ref('raw_compensation') }}
),

renamed as (
    select
        -- Foreign key to stg_employees
        employee_id,

        -- Event identity
        effective_date,
        change_reason,

        -- Compensation snapshot at this effective_date
        salary,
        comp_band_min,
        comp_band_mid,
        comp_band_max
    from source
)

select * from renamed

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_employees.name: stg_employees
model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_employees.description:
Staging view over raw_employees (ADP). Cleans and types the seeded
CSV. Calculated fields (tenure_months, is_active, compa_ratio,
has_recent_promotion) are layered downstream in intermediate / marts.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description: Unique identifier assigned at hire. Format EMP-NNNN. Primary key.

manager_id.name: manager_id
manager_id.description:
The employee_id of this employee's direct manager. Null only
for the CEO (EMP-001 Maya Chen, who reports to the Board).
Self-referential FK to stg_employees.employee_id.

first_name.name: first_name
first_name.description: Legal first name as recorded in the HRIS.

last_name.name: last_name
last_name.description: Legal last name as recorded in the HRIS.

email.name: email
email.description:
Company-issued work email address. Deactivated upon
termination. Globally unique across active and terminated
rows; numeric suffix appended on name collisions
(e.g. priya.smith2@justkaizen.ai).

department.name: department
department.description:
Primary organizational department. One of seven standard
departments plus 'Executive' (CEO-only).

sub_department.name: sub_department
sub_department.description:
Functional sub-group within the department. Null only for
the CEO (Executive department has no sub-department slot).
Leadership-only sub-departments are wrapped in parentheses
(e.g. '(CTO)', '(VP Sales)') to distinguish them from
regular sub-departments staffed by ICs and managers.

job_title.name: job_title
job_title.description:
Current job title. For leadership uses the Section 2 spec
title verbatim (e.g. 'VP Engineering, Platform'); for non-
leadership uses the Job Architecture title for the
(department, sub_department, job_level) cell.

job_level.name: job_level
job_level.description:
Standardized job level. IC1-IC5 for individual contributors,
M1-M5 for managers and executives.

hire_date.name: hire_date
hire_date.description: The official start date of employment.

termination_date.name: termination_date
termination_date.description:
Date employment ended. Null for active employees. When
populated, always strictly greater than hire_date (validated
upstream in the Python coherence checks).

employment_status.name: employment_status
employment_status.description:
Active (currently employed) or Terminated (departed). Derived
from termination_date in the source pipeline; tracked
explicitly for fast filtering.

termination_type.name: termination_type
termination_type.description:
Classification of how employment ended. One of Voluntary,
Involuntary, or Layoff. Null for active employees.

termination_reason.name: termination_reason
termination_reason.description:
Coded departure reason recorded by People Ops during
offboarding. Null for active employees. Reason values are
archetype-driven (e.g. layoff casualties carry 'Reduction in
Force'; manager change casualties carry 'Manager Relationship'
50% / 'Company Culture' 30% / 'Career Opportunity' 20%).

location_city.name: location_city
location_city.description:
The city where the employee is primarily based. All employees
are remote-first; home city tracked for tax and compliance.

location_state.name: location_state
location_state.description:
The US state code (or international country) where the
employee is based.

gender.name: gender
gender.description:
Self-reported gender identity collected during onboarding.
Used for DEIB reporting only.

race_ethnicity.name: race_ethnicity
race_ethnicity.description:
Self-reported race / ethnicity collected during onboarding.
Used for DEIB reporting only.

is_critical_talent.name: is_critical_talent
is_critical_talent.description:
People Ops flag indicating whether leadership has designated
this employee as critical to retain. Updated quarterly during
talent review. Always true for active leadership and active
founders (per Section 5); ~40% of active high-flyers; false
for everyone else.

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_employees.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        stg_employees
    Layer:        staging
    Source:       seeds/raw_employees.csv  (ADP - Core Employee Records)
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml staging defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Type-cast and lightly clean the seeded raw_employees CSV. Output is a
    1:1 typed view of the source rows. No business logic, joins, or
    calculated fields are introduced in staging; downstream models in
    intermediate/ and marts/ derive tenure_months, is_active, compa_ratio,
    has_recent_promotion, and other analyst-facing measures.

    Source dependencies
    -------------------
    - seeds/raw_employees.csv (loaded into the `raw` dataset by `dbt seed`)

    Business rules applied
    ----------------------
    - is_critical_talent receives an explicit BOOL cast (defensive; the
      seed column_types in dbt_project.yml already enforce BOOL on load,
      but the cast here documents intent).
    - hire_date and termination_date are passed through unchanged. Their
      type is fixed to DATE by the seed config.
    - All STRING fields pass through as-is. Section 12 cross-table
      coherence is validated in the upstream Python pipeline
      (scripts/data_generation/13_validate_and_export.py); this model
      trusts the loaded shape.
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with source as (
    select * from {{ ref('raw_employees') }}
),

renamed as (
    select
        -- Identifiers
        employee_id,
        manager_id,

        -- Personal
        first_name,
        last_name,
        email,

        -- Org placement
        department,
        sub_department,
        job_title,
        job_level,

        -- Employment timeline
        hire_date,
        termination_date,
        employment_status,
        termination_type,
        termination_reason,

        -- Demographics (DEIB reporting only)
        gender,
        race_ethnicity,

        -- Location (US-only, remote-first)
        location_city,
        location_state,

        -- Talent designation (People Ops flag, refreshed quarterly)
        cast(is_critical_talent as bool) as is_critical_talent
    from source
)

select * from renamed

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_engagemen.name: stg_engagement
model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_engagemen.description:
Staging view over raw_engagement (Lattice, ANONYMIZED). One row
per (survey_cycle, department, sub_department, question_id).
No employee_id field by design -- all metrics aggregated at the
department level to preserve anonymity. The CEO (Maya, Executive
department) is excluded from aggregation since the dept has only
one employee. Calculated rollups (theme averages, cycle-over-
cycle deltas, top-quartile flags) derive in intermediate / marts.

survey_cycle.name: survey_cycle
survey_cycle.description:
Quarterly survey identifier. Format YYYY-QN (e.g. 2024-Q3).
Surveys run quarterly: Q1 (Mar), Q2 (Jun), Q3 (Sep), Q4 (Dec).
First cycle: 2021-Q2. Last cycle in this dataset: 2025-Q1.

department.name: department
department.description:
Department the aggregated responses belong to. The CEO
(Executive department) is excluded -- a department of one
would violate anonymization.

sub_department.name: sub_department
sub_department.description:
Sub-department for more granular analysis. Populated only
when the sub-dept has 5+ respondents per Section 12 anonymity
rule. Always null in this dataset (the current build emits
only dept-level rows); the column is present for forward
compatibility with future runs that include sub-dept detail.

question_id.name: question_id
question_id.description:
Question identifier. Format theme abbreviation + sequence
number (e.g. MGE3). 27 standard questions across 8 themes
per Section 10.

question_text.name: question_text
question_text.description: Full text of the survey question as presented to employees.

theme.name: theme
theme.description:
Thematic category the question belongs to. Used for grouping
related questions in analysis.

avg_score.name: avg_score
avg_score.description:
Mean response score across all respondents in the department
for this question. Scale: 1 (Strongly Disagree) to 5 (Strongly
Agree). Always between 1.0 and 5.0 inclusive.

favorable_pct.name: favorable_pct
favorable_pct.description:
Percentage of respondents who selected 4 (Agree) or 5
(Strongly Agree). Stored as a decimal between 0 and 1.
Multiply by 100 in downstream models for display.

response_count.name: response_count
response_count.description:
Number of employees in the department who answered this
question. Always 78%-88% of the department's active headcount
at the survey cycle date (Section 12 HR19, HR20). The same
value applies across every question_id within a (cycle,
department) since the survey is taken once per respondent.

enps_score.name: enps_score
enps_score.description:
Employee Net Promoter Score for this (department, cycle).
Calculated as % Promoters (9-10) minus % Detractors (0-6) on
a 0-10 scale. Range: -100 to 100. Denormalized -- same value
across every row that shares (survey_cycle, department).

model.justkaizen_analytics.stg_engagemen.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        stg_engagement
    Layer:        staging
    Source:       seeds/raw_engagement.csv  (Lattice - Engagement Survey, ANONYMIZED)
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml staging defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Type-cast and lightly clean the seeded raw_engagement CSV. One row
    per (survey_cycle, department, sub_department, question_id). Output
    is a typed 1:1 view of the source rows; calculated theme rollups,
    cycle-over-cycle trend deltas, and question rollups derive in
    intermediate / marts.

    Privacy
    -------
    This table is anonymized -- no employee_id field exists. All metrics
    are aggregated at the (cycle, department) granularity, with optional
    sub-department detail when the sub-dept has 5+ respondents per
    Section 12 anonymity rule. Sub-department rows are not generated in
    this dataset; the column is present for forward compatibility.

    Source dependencies
    -------------------
    - seeds/raw_engagement.csv (loaded into raw.raw_engagement by dbt seed)

    Business rules applied
    ----------------------
    - Maya Chen (the sole Executive department employee) is excluded
      from the per-(cycle, department) aggregation by design --
      reporting on a single person would violate anonymization.
    - response_count is selected as INT64 to fall in the integer range
      [ceil(0.78 * active_count), floor(0.88 * active_count)] per
      Section 12 HR20. For the smallest departments where the integer
      rate is unattainable, the closest integer to 0.83 * active_count
      is used.
    - enps_score is the same value across every row that shares
      (survey_cycle, department) -- denormalized per the schema.
    - Section 12 HR19 enforces response_count <= active dept headcount;
      HR20 enforces the 78-88% rate.

    Calculated fields routed downstream
    -----------------------------------
        favorable_pct_pct       favorable_pct * 100 for percentage display
        score_vs_baseline       avg_score - company-wide baseline (Section 10)
        cycle_over_cycle_delta  current cycle's avg_score minus prior cycle's
        is_top_quartile         within-theme percentile flag
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with source as (
    select * from {{ ref('raw_engagement') }}
),

renamed as (
    select
        -- Cycle + dimension identity
        survey_cycle,
        department,
        sub_department,

        -- Question identity
        question_id,
        question_text,
        theme,

        -- Aggregate metrics
        avg_score,
        favorable_pct,
        response_count,

        -- Department-level eNPS (denormalized -- same value across all
        -- rows that share survey_cycle + department)
        enps_score
    from source
)

select * from renamed

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_compensat.name: fct_compensation_parity
model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_compensat.description:
Per-active-employee compensation snapshot with peer-cohort
percentiles for pay parity analysis. Cohort is defined as
(department, job_level). Granularity: one row per active
employee.

compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level.name: compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level
compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level.description: PERCENT_RANK over (department, job_level) ordered by compa_ratio.

cohort_size.name: cohort_size
cohort_size.description: Count of active employees in the (department, job_level) cohort.

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_compensat.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        fct_compensation_parity
    Layer:        marts
    Sources:      int_employee_dimension
    Materialized: table  (per dbt_project.yml marts defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Per-active-employee compensation snapshot with peer-group
    percentiles. Granularity: one row per active employee. The peer
    group for ranking is (department, job_level) -- the natural
    cohort within which compa_ratio comparison is fair.

    Filters
    -------
    Active employees only. Comp parity for terminated employees
    isn't a useful analytical view; the underlying comp history is
    still in stg_compensation if needed.

    Calculated fields (added on top of int_employee_dimension)
    ----------------------------------------------------------
        compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level
            PERCENT_RANK over (department, job_level). Range [0, 1].
            0 = lowest compa_ratio in cohort; 1 = highest.

        salary_percentile_within_dept_level
            Same shape on raw salary.

        cohort_avg_compa_ratio                  AVG over (dept, level)
        cohort_avg_salary                       AVG over (dept, level)
        cohort_size                             COUNT over (dept, level)

        compa_ratio_vs_cohort_avg               compa_ratio - cohort_avg_compa_ratio
        salary_vs_cohort_avg                    salary - cohort_avg_salary

        compa_ratio_quartile_label
            'Bottom Quartile' / 'Q2' / 'Q3' / 'Top Quartile' from the
            percentile.

    These let dashboards pivot on (gender, race_ethnicity, location_state)
    within the (dept, level) cohort to surface pay-parity gaps without
    further aggregation.
*/

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

with active_employees as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_employee_dimension') }}
    where is_active
),

with_cohort_metrics as (
    select
        *,
        percent_rank() over (
            partition by department, job_level
            order by compa_ratio
        ) as compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level,

        percent_rank() over (
            partition by department, job_level
            order by salary
        ) as salary_percentile_within_dept_level,

        avg(compa_ratio) over (
            partition by department, job_level
        ) as cohort_avg_compa_ratio,

        avg(salary) over (
            partition by department, job_level
        ) as cohort_avg_salary,

        count(*) over (
            partition by department, job_level
        ) as cohort_size
    from active_employees
),

final as (
    select
        -- Identity
        employee_id,
        first_name,
        last_name,
        email,

        -- Cohort dimensions
        department,
        sub_department,
        job_level,
        job_title,

        -- Demographics for parity slicing
        gender,
        race_ethnicity,
        location_city,
        location_state,
        is_critical_talent,

        -- Tenure context
        hire_date,
        tenure_years,
        total_promotions,

        -- Comp snapshot
        salary,
        comp_band_min,
        comp_band_mid,
        comp_band_max,
        compa_ratio,
        band_position,
        current_comp_change_reason,
        current_comp_effective_date,

        -- Peer-cohort rank
        round(compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level, 4) as compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level,
        round(salary_percentile_within_dept_level,      4) as salary_percentile_within_dept_level,
        round(cohort_avg_compa_ratio, 4)                   as cohort_avg_compa_ratio,
        round(cohort_avg_salary,      0)                   as cohort_avg_salary,
        cohort_size,

        -- Distance from cohort average
        round(compa_ratio - cohort_avg_compa_ratio, 4) as compa_ratio_vs_cohort_avg,
        round(salary - cohort_avg_salary,           0) as salary_vs_cohort_avg,

        case
            when compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level >= 0.75 then 'Top Quartile'
            when compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level >= 0.50 then 'Q3'
            when compa_ratio_percentile_within_dept_level >= 0.25 then 'Q2'
            else 'Bottom Quartile'
        end as compa_ratio_quartile_label
    from with_cohort_metrics
)

select * from final

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_performan.name: fct_performance_distribution
model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_performan.description:
Per (employee, review_cycle) wide row carrying ratings + trend
signals + current dimension context (dept, level, demographics,
tenure, comp). Granularity matches int_employee_performance_history.
Note: dept / level / comp are CURRENT, not at-time-of-cycle.
model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_performan.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        fct_performance_distribution
    Layer:        marts
    Sources:      int_employee_performance_history, int_employee_dimension
    Materialized: table  (per dbt_project.yml marts defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Per (employee, review_cycle) wide row with all dims needed to
    slice the performance distribution: dept, level, demographics,
    tenure, comp. Granularity matches int_employee_performance_history
    (~2,676 rows).

    Caveat: dept / level / comp dimensions are CURRENT (snapshot from
    int_employee_dimension), not at-time-of-cycle. For most dashboards
    this is the right framing -- analysts ask "what's the rating
    distribution for current Senior Software Engineers" rather than
    "...for who was a Senior Software Engineer in 2022-H2". A true
    historical snapshot would require pivoting stg_job_history to a
    state-at-date function (deferred).

    Calculated fields (added beyond int_employee_performance_history)
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
        cycle_year                    extract(year from cycle_end_date)
        cycle_half                    'H1' or 'H2'
        rating_distribution_quartile  bucket of overall_rating_numeric for
                                      easy bar-chart bucketing in BI
*/

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

with performance as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_employee_performance_history') }}
),

employees as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_employee_dimension') }}
),

final as (
    select
        -- Cycle identity
        p.review_cycle,
        p.cycle_end_date,
        extract(year from p.cycle_end_date) as cycle_year,
        case
            when p.review_cycle like '%-H1' then 'H1'
            else 'H2'
        end as cycle_half,
        p.cycle_sequence,
        p.review_completed_date,
        p.review_status,

        -- Employee identity
        p.employee_id,
        e.first_name,
        e.last_name,

        -- Org context (CURRENT, not at-time-of-cycle)
        e.department,
        e.sub_department,
        e.job_level,
        e.job_title,
        e.manager_id,
        e.is_active,

        -- Demographics
        e.gender,
        e.race_ethnicity,
        e.location_state,
        e.is_critical_talent,

        -- Tenure context at the snapshot date
        e.tenure_years,
        e.total_promotions,
        e.career_velocity_per_year,

        -- Compensation context
        e.salary,
        e.compa_ratio,
        e.band_position,

        -- Ratings
        p.overall_rating,
        p.overall_rating_numeric,
        p.manager_rating,
        p.manager_rating_numeric,
        p.self_rating,
        p.self_rating_numeric,

        -- Trend signals
        p.prior_overall_rating,
        p.prior_overall_rating_numeric,
        p.overall_rating_delta,
        p.is_top_performer,
        p.self_optimism_delta,

        -- Bar-chart bucket
        case p.overall_rating
            when 'Significantly Exceeds' then 'High'
            when 'Exceeds'                then 'High'
            when 'Meets'                  then 'On Target'
            when 'Partially Meets'        then 'Below'
            when 'Does Not Meet'          then 'Below'
        end as rating_distribution_band
    from performance as p
    left join employees as e on p.employee_id = e.employee_id
)

select * from final

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_workforce.name: fct_workforce_overview
model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_workforce.description:
Headline workforce time series. One row per (department,
year_quarter) across 21 quarters x 7 departments = 147 rows.
Carries start / end headcount snapshots, period flow (hires,
terminations split by type), net change, average period
headcount, and attrition rate. Q1 2023 layoff is visible in
the layoff_terminations column; the Section 1 hypergrowth peak
(2022-Q2) is visible in hires_in_period.

hires_in_period.name: hires_in_period
hires_in_period.description:
Count of employees with hire_date between quarter_start_date
and quarter_end_date (inclusive).

terminations_in_period.name: terminations_in_period
terminations_in_period.description:
Count with termination_date between quarter_start_date and
quarter_end_date.

layoff_terminations.name: layoff_terminations
layoff_terminations.description:
Layoff-type terminations only. The Q1 2023 row across
departments should sum to 75 per Section 1 spec.

attrition_rate_pct.name: attrition_rate_pct
attrition_rate_pct.description:
terminations_in_period / avg_period_headcount * 100. NULL
when avg_period_headcount is 0 (departments before they had
any employees).

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_workforce.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        fct_workforce_overview
    Layer:        marts
    Sources:      int_employee_dimension, stg_employees
    Materialized: table  (per dbt_project.yml marts defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Headline workforce time series for the executive dashboard. One row
    per (department, year_quarter). Period spans 2020-Q1 through 2025-Q1
    (21 quarters x 7 departments = 147 rows).

    Metrics
    -------
        start_headcount             active at the day before the quarter starts
        end_headcount               active at quarter_end_date
        net_change                  end - start
        hires_in_period             count of employees with hire_date in [start, end]
        terminations_in_period      count with termination_date in [start, end]
        voluntary_terminations
        involuntary_terminations
        layoff_terminations         (split of terminations_in_period by type)
        attrition_rate_pct          terminations / avg_period_headcount * 100
        avg_period_headcount        (start + end) / 2

    "Active on date X" = hire_date <= X AND (termination_date is null
    OR termination_date >= X). This treats termination_date as the
    employee's last day worked.

    Notable signals visible in this fact
    ------------------------------------
    - Q1 2023 layoff: 75 layoff_terminations across Sales (25),
      Engineering (20), G&A (12), Marketing (8), CS (5), People (5)
      with Product = 0 (Section 1 layoff distribution).
    - Hypergrowth peak: 2022-Q2 with ~80 hires_in_period across all
      departments (Section 1 headcount timeline).
    - Hiring freeze: 2023-Q1 / 2023-Q2 with 0 hires (post-layoff).
*/

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

with employees as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_employee_dimension') }}
),

quarters as (
    select
        quarter_start,
        date_sub(date_add(quarter_start, interval 1 quarter), interval 1 day) as quarter_end,
        date_sub(quarter_start, interval 1 day)                               as day_before_quarter,
        concat(
            cast(extract(year from quarter_start) as string),
            '-Q',
            cast(extract(quarter from quarter_start) as string)
        ) as year_quarter
    from unnest(
        generate_date_array(
            date '2020-01-01',
            date '2025-01-01',
            interval 1 quarter
        )
    ) as quarter_start
),

departments as (
    select distinct department
    from employees
    where department in (
        'Engineering', 'Sales', 'Customer Success', 'Marketing',
        'Product', 'G&A', 'People'
    )
),

quarter_dept_grid as (
    select
        q.year_quarter,
        q.quarter_start,
        q.quarter_end,
        q.day_before_quarter,
        d.department
    from quarters as q
    cross join departments as d
),

with_metrics as (
    select
        g.year_quarter,
        g.quarter_start,
        g.quarter_end,
        g.department,

        -- Headcount snapshots
        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.hire_date <= g.day_before_quarter
            and (e.termination_date is null or e.termination_date >= g.day_before_quarter)
        ) as start_headcount,

        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.hire_date <= g.quarter_end
            and (e.termination_date is null or e.termination_date >= g.quarter_end)
        ) as end_headcount,

        -- Period flow
        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.hire_date between g.quarter_start and g.quarter_end
        ) as hires_in_period,

        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.termination_date between g.quarter_start and g.quarter_end
        ) as terminations_in_period,

        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.termination_date between g.quarter_start and g.quarter_end
            and e.termination_type = 'Voluntary'
        ) as voluntary_terminations,

        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.termination_date between g.quarter_start and g.quarter_end
            and e.termination_type = 'Involuntary'
        ) as involuntary_terminations,

        countif(
            e.department = g.department
            and e.termination_date between g.quarter_start and g.quarter_end
            and e.termination_type = 'Layoff'
        ) as layoff_terminations
    from quarter_dept_grid as g
    left join employees       as e on true
    group by
        g.year_quarter,
        g.quarter_start,
        g.quarter_end,
        g.department
),

final as (
    select
        year_quarter,
        quarter_start                              as quarter_start_date,
        quarter_end                                as quarter_end_date,
        department,

        start_headcount,
        end_headcount,
        end_headcount - start_headcount            as net_change,
        round((start_headcount + end_headcount) / 2.0, 1) as avg_period_headcount,

        hires_in_period,
        terminations_in_period,
        voluntary_terminations,
        involuntary_terminations,
        layoff_terminations,

        round(
            safe_divide(
                terminations_in_period * 100.0,
                (start_headcount + end_headcount) / 2.0
            ),
            2
        ) as attrition_rate_pct
    from with_metrics
)

select * from final
order by quarter_start_date, department

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_attrition.name: fct_attrition_drivers
model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_attrition.description:
One row per terminated employee with attribution columns
explaining why they left. terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change
surfaces the Manager Change Casualty archetype's defining pattern;
was_declining_performer surfaces the Performance Managed Out
archetype's pattern.

terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change.name: terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change
terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change.description:
TRUE when the employee had a Manager Change event within 6
months of their termination_date. Manager Change Casualty
archetype's defining flag.

was_declining_performer.name: was_declining_performer
was_declining_performer.description:
TRUE when the last review's overall_rating_delta is negative.
Performance Managed Out archetype's defining flag.

model.justkaizen_analytics.fct_attrition.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        fct_attrition_drivers
    Layer:        marts
    Sources:      int_employee_dimension
                  int_employee_event_sequence
                  int_employee_performance_history
    Materialized: table  (per dbt_project.yml marts defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    One row per terminated employee (~224 rows) with attribution
    columns the analytics team uses to explain why people leave:
    tenure at termination, time since last promotion, recent manager
    change, performance trajectory, and a heuristic
    `terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change` flag (matches the
    Manager Change Casualty archetype's defining pattern per Section 5).

    Calculated fields
    -----------------
        tenure_at_termination_months    months from hire_date to
                                          termination_date
        last_manager_change_before_term  most recent Manager Change
                                          event whose effective_date <
                                          termination_date
        months_since_last_manager_change_at_term
                                          months from that event to
                                          termination_date
        terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change
                                          TRUE if the gap above is <= 6
                                          months. Surfaces the Manager
                                          Change Casualty pattern.
        last_review_rating_at_term       overall_rating from the last
                                          review prior to termination
        last_review_rating_numeric_at_term
                                          numeric mapping
        was_declining_performer
                                          TRUE if the last review's
                                          overall_rating_delta < 0
                                          (numeric drop). Surfaces the
                                          Performance Managed Out
                                          pattern.
*/

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

with terminated_employees as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_employee_dimension') }}
    where employment_status = 'Terminated'
),

manager_change_events as (
    select *
    from {{ ref('int_employee_event_sequence') }}
    where change_type = 'Manager Change'
),

last_manager_change_per_employee as (
    select
        emp.employee_id,
        max(mc.effective_date) as last_manager_change_before_term
    from terminated_employees as emp
    left join manager_change_events as mc
      on emp.employee_id = mc.employee_id
     and mc.effective_date < emp.termination_date
    group by emp.employee_id
),

last_review_before_term as (
    select *
    from {{ ref('int_employee_performance_history') }} as p
    where exists (
        select 1
        from terminated_employees as e
        where e.employee_id = p.employee_id
          and p.review_completed_date <= e.termination_date
    )
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by employee_id
        order by review_completed_date desc, cycle_sequence desc
    ) = 1
),

final as (
    select
        -- Identity
        e.employee_id,
        e.first_name,
        e.last_name,

        -- Dept / level / demographics at termination snapshot
        e.department,
        e.sub_department,
        e.job_level,
        e.gender,
        e.race_ethnicity,
        e.location_state,
        e.is_critical_talent,

        -- Termination details
        e.hire_date,
        e.termination_date,
        e.termination_type,
        e.termination_reason,
        date_diff(e.termination_date, e.hire_date, month) as tenure_at_termination_months,
        round(date_diff(e.termination_date, e.hire_date, day) / 365.25, 2) as tenure_at_termination_years,

        -- Promotion context
        e.total_promotions,
        e.last_promotion_date,
        e.months_since_last_promotion as months_since_last_promotion_at_term,

        -- Manager change context
        mcr.last_manager_change_before_term,
        case
            when mcr.last_manager_change_before_term is not null
                then date_diff(e.termination_date, mcr.last_manager_change_before_term, month)
            else null
        end as months_since_last_manager_change_at_term,
        case
            when mcr.last_manager_change_before_term is not null
                and date_diff(e.termination_date, mcr.last_manager_change_before_term, month) <= 6
                then true
            else false
        end as terminated_within_6mo_of_manager_change,

        -- Performance context
        lrb.review_cycle           as last_review_cycle_at_term,
        lrb.overall_rating         as last_review_rating_at_term,
        lrb.overall_rating_numeric as last_review_rating_numeric_at_term,
        lrb.overall_rating_delta   as last_review_rating_delta_at_term,
        case
            when lrb.overall_rating_delta is not null
                and lrb.overall_rating_delta < 0
                then true
            else false
        end as was_declining_performer,

        -- Comp at last record (forward-carried in int_employee_dimension)
        e.salary,
        e.compa_ratio,
        e.band_position
    from terminated_employees                  as e
    left join last_manager_change_per_employee as mcr on e.employee_id = mcr.employee_id
    left join last_review_before_term          as lrb on e.employee_id = lrb.employee_id
)

select * from final
order by termination_date desc

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.name: int_employee_compensation_current
model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.description:
One row per employee with their most-recent compensation record
plus the spec's two calculated metrics (compa_ratio and
band_position). "Most recent" is the row with the latest
effective_date in stg_compensation; for terminated employees this
is whatever record was active at termination (Section 12 HR8
prohibits records after termination_date).

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description: FK to stg_employees. One row per employee. Primary key.

current_comp_effective_date.name: current_comp_effective_date
current_comp_effective_date.description:
Effective date of the latest comp record for this employee.
Always >= hire_date and <= reference_date in
int_employee_tenure.

current_comp_change_reason.name: current_comp_change_reason
current_comp_change_reason.description:
The change_reason of the latest comp record. Indicates the
source of the current salary value (e.g. 'Annual Review',
'Promotion', 'Market Adjustment', or 'New Hire' for employees
who have never had a subsequent comp event).

salary.name: salary
salary.description:
Annual base salary in USD as of current_comp_effective_date.
Always within [comp_band_min, comp_band_max] (Section 12 HR7).

comp_band_min.name: comp_band_min
comp_band_min.description:
Minimum of the function-specific comp band for the employee's
(department, sub_department, job_level) at
current_comp_effective_date.

comp_band_mid.name: comp_band_mid
comp_band_mid.description: Midpoint of the comp band. Denominator for compa_ratio.

comp_band_max.name: comp_band_max
comp_band_max.description: Maximum of the comp band. Salary is clipped to this ceiling.

compa_ratio.name: compa_ratio
compa_ratio.description:
salary / comp_band_mid, rounded to 4 decimals. Typical range
0.85 to 1.20 since salary is bounded to [band_min, band_max].
A compa_ratio < 1.00 indicates below-midpoint pay; > 1.00
above-midpoint. Section 5 archetype rules anchor target
ranges (e.g. High-flyer 1.05-1.15, Steady contributor
0.95-1.05, Founder 1.10-1.20).

band_position.name: band_position
band_position.description:
(salary - comp_band_min) / (comp_band_max - comp_band_min),
rounded to 4 decimals. Range [0, 1]. 0 = at band minimum,
0.5 = at midpoint, 1 = at band ceiling. Useful for spotting
employees compressed against the band ceiling who may need a
band re-evaluation or promotion to grow further.

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        int_employee_compensation_current
    Layer:        intermediate
    Sources:      stg_compensation
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml intermediate defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    One row per employee with their most recent compensation snapshot
    plus the spec's two calculated comp metrics. "Most recent" means
    the row with the latest effective_date in stg_compensation; for
    terminated employees this is whatever record was active at
    termination, since Section 12 HR8 prohibits comp records after
    termination_date.

    Calculated fields
    -----------------
        compa_ratio    salary / comp_band_mid (per data dict)
                       Rounded to 4 decimals. Typical range 0.85 - 1.20
                       since salary is clipped to [band_min, band_max].
        band_position  (salary - comp_band_min) / (comp_band_max - comp_band_min)
                       Range [0, 1]. 0 = at band minimum, 1 = at band ceiling.
                       Uses SAFE_DIVIDE to handle the edge case where
                       comp_band_min == comp_band_max (no rows in this
                       dataset, but defensive).

    Edge cases
    ----------
    - Employees with a single comp record (the New Hire row) get that
      row as "latest" -- no ambiguity.
    - Same-day comp records (e.g. Promotion + Annual Review on the same
      Jan 15) are deduplicated by row_number with a deterministic
      tiebreaker on change_reason for reproducibility.
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with comp as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_compensation') }}
),

ranked as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (
            partition by employee_id
            order by effective_date desc, change_reason
        ) as rn
    from comp
),

latest as (
    select * except (rn)
    from ranked
    where rn = 1
),

final as (
    select
        employee_id,

        -- Provenance of the snapshot
        effective_date as current_comp_effective_date,
        change_reason  as current_comp_change_reason,

        -- Compensation snapshot
        salary,
        comp_band_min,
        comp_band_mid,
        comp_band_max,

        -- Calculated metrics
        round(salary / comp_band_mid, 4) as compa_ratio,
        round(
            safe_divide(salary - comp_band_min, comp_band_max - comp_band_min),
            4
        ) as band_position
    from latest
)

select * from final

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.name: int_employee_event_sequence
model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.description:
Chronologically ordered career-event stream with prev / next event
metadata per row. Same row count and granularity as
stg_job_history (~1,045 rows) plus six derived columns:
event_sequence, total_events_for_employee, is_latest_event,
prev_event_date / type, next_event_date / type,
days_since_prev_event, days_until_next_event. Used by marts that
need time-between calculations (e.g., promotion cadence, time-
between-manager-changes) and by the latest-event filter
(is_latest_event = true) for dimension lookups.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description:
FK to stg_employees. Every employee has at least one row
(their Hire event); count of rows per employee equals
total_events_for_employee.

event_sequence.name: event_sequence
event_sequence.description:
1-based ordinal of this event within the employee's history,
ordered by (effective_date, change_type). event_sequence = 1
is always the Hire event.

total_events_for_employee.name: total_events_for_employee
total_events_for_employee.description:
Total number of rows for this employee in this model
(denormalized; same value across every row that shares
employee_id). Equal to max(event_sequence) per employee.

is_latest_event.name: is_latest_event
is_latest_event.description:
TRUE when event_sequence = total_events_for_employee (the
most recent event for the employee). FALSE otherwise. Useful
for filtering to current-state lookups without joining back
to stg_employees.

prev_event_date.name: prev_event_date
prev_event_date.description:
effective_date of the immediately preceding event for this
employee, ordered by event_sequence. Null on event_sequence = 1
(the Hire row).

prev_event_type.name: prev_event_type
prev_event_type.description:
change_type of the immediately preceding event. Null on
event_sequence = 1.

next_event_date.name: next_event_date
next_event_date.description:
effective_date of the immediately following event for this
employee. Null on the latest event (is_latest_event = TRUE).

next_event_type.name: next_event_type
next_event_type.description:
change_type of the immediately following event. Null on the
latest event.

days_since_prev_event.name: days_since_prev_event
days_since_prev_event.description:
effective_date minus prev_event_date in days. Always >= 0
when populated (events are chronologically ordered). Null on
event_sequence = 1.

days_until_next_event.name: days_until_next_event
days_until_next_event.description:
next_event_date minus effective_date in days. Always >= 0 when
populated. Null on the latest event for an employee.

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        int_employee_event_sequence
    Layer:        intermediate
    Sources:      stg_job_history
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml intermediate defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Chronologically ordered career-event stream with prev / next event
    metadata per row. One row per (employee_id, event) -- the same
    granularity as stg_job_history -- enriched with window calculations
    that downstream marts and the org-evolution dashboard need:

    - event_sequence:           1-based ordinal per employee (Hire = 1)
    - is_latest_event:          flag on the most recent row per employee
    - prev_event_date / type:   the immediately preceding event
    - next_event_date / type:   the immediately following event
    - days_since_prev_event:    interval to the previous event
    - days_until_next_event:    interval to the next event

    Tiebreaker on same-date events
    ------------------------------
    Ordering uses (effective_date, change_type) so same-day events are
    deterministic. 'Hire' sorts first alphabetically -- which is the
    correct semantic placement since the Hire event is always the first
    event for an employee. Other same-day events (Promotion + Manager
    Change is the most common collision) get an alphabetical order;
    this is arbitrary but stable across runs.

    What this model does NOT compute
    --------------------------------
    Per-employee aggregates (total_promotions, last_promotion_date,
    has_manager_change_last_12mo, career_velocity_per_year) live in
    int_employee_tenure. This model preserves row-level granularity so
    downstream consumers can filter / pivot to whichever event subset
    they need.
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with events as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_job_history') }}
),

with_ordering as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (
            partition by employee_id
            order by effective_date, change_type
        ) as event_sequence,
        count(*) over (
            partition by employee_id
        ) as total_events_for_employee
    from events
),

with_neighbors as (
    select
        *,

        -- Previous event
        lag(effective_date) over (
            partition by employee_id
            order by event_sequence
        ) as prev_event_date,
        lag(change_type) over (
            partition by employee_id
            order by event_sequence
        ) as prev_event_type,

        -- Next event
        lead(effective_date) over (
            partition by employee_id
            order by event_sequence
        ) as next_event_date,
        lead(change_type) over (
            partition by employee_id
            order by event_sequence
        ) as next_event_type
    from with_ordering
),

final as (
    select
        -- Identity & ordering
        employee_id,
        effective_date,
        change_type,
        event_sequence,
        total_events_for_employee,
        case
            when event_sequence = total_events_for_employee then true
            else false
        end as is_latest_event,

        -- Old / new state (carried forward from stg_job_history)
        old_job_level,
        new_job_level,
        old_department,
        new_department,
        old_sub_department,
        new_sub_department,
        old_job_title,
        new_job_title,
        old_manager_id,
        new_manager_id,

        -- Neighbor metadata
        prev_event_date,
        prev_event_type,
        next_event_date,
        next_event_type,

        -- Time gaps
        case
            when prev_event_date is not null
                then date_diff(effective_date, prev_event_date, day)
            else null
        end as days_since_prev_event,
        case
            when next_event_date is not null
                then date_diff(next_event_date, effective_date, day)
            else null
        end as days_until_next_event
    from with_neighbors
)

select * from final

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_engagemen.name: int_engagement_theme_rollup
model.justkaizen_analytics.int_engagemen.description:
The 27 standard engagement questions rolled up to the 8 theme
level per (survey_cycle, department, sub_department). One row per
(cycle, dept, sub_dept, theme); compound key in practice is
(cycle, dept, theme) since sub_department is null on every row in
the current build (raw_engagement emits dept-level only). Adds
cycle_end_date for chronological ordering plus prior-cycle and
delta columns for trend dashboards.

cycle_end_date.name: cycle_end_date
cycle_end_date.description:
Calendar date the cycle's quarter closed. Q1 -> Mar 31,
Q2 -> Jun 30, Q3 -> Sep 30, Q4 -> Dec 31. Used as the
ordering key for cycle-over-cycle trend windows.

sub_department.name: sub_department
sub_department.description:
Always null in this build. Reserved for forward compatibility
when the generator emits sub-department rows for sub-depts
with 5+ respondents.

questions_in_theme.name: questions_in_theme
questions_in_theme.description:
Count of question rows rolled into this theme aggregate.
Should equal the number of standard questions defined for
the theme (4 for ENG, 5 for MGE, 3 for the rest).

response_count.name: response_count
response_count.description:
Carried forward from stg_engagement. Same value across every
row in the same (cycle, dept) since the survey is taken once
per respondent.

theme_avg_score.name: theme_avg_score
theme_avg_score.description:
Mean of avg_score across the questions in this theme.
Range [1, 5] on the Likert scale.

theme_avg_favorable_pct.name: theme_avg_favorable_pct
theme_avg_favorable_pct.description:
Mean of favorable_pct across the questions in this theme.
Range [0, 1].

prior_theme_avg_score.name: prior_theme_avg_score
prior_theme_avg_score.description:
theme_avg_score from the immediately preceding cycle for the
same (department, theme). Null on the first cycle a
(dept, theme) appears.

prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct.name: prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct
prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct.description:
theme_avg_favorable_pct from the immediately preceding cycle.
Null on the first appearance.

theme_score_delta.name: theme_score_delta
theme_score_delta.description:
theme_avg_score - prior_theme_avg_score, rounded to 2
decimals. Range [-4, 4]. Null on the first cycle.

theme_favorable_pct_delta.name: theme_favorable_pct_delta
theme_favorable_pct_delta.description:
theme_avg_favorable_pct - prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct,
rounded to 4 decimals. Range [-1, 1]. Null on the first cycle.

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_engagemen.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        int_engagement_theme_rollup
    Layer:        intermediate
    Sources:      stg_engagement
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml intermediate defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Roll the 27 standard engagement questions up to the 8 theme level
    so dashboards can chart "Manager Effectiveness over time" without
    aggregating five MGE rows per cycle in the BI tool. One row per
    (survey_cycle, department, sub_department, theme).

    Calculated fields
    -----------------
        theme_avg_score              mean of avg_score across questions in the theme
        theme_avg_favorable_pct      mean of favorable_pct across questions in the theme
        questions_in_theme           count of questions rolled into this theme row
        cycle_end_date               parsed from survey_cycle (Q1 -> Mar 31, etc.)
        prior_theme_avg_score        lag for cycle-over-cycle trend
        theme_score_delta            theme_avg_score - prior_theme_avg_score
        prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct, theme_favorable_pct_delta
                                     same trend pattern for the favorable percentage

    response_count and enps_score are denormalized per (cycle, dept) in
    stg_engagement -- the same values across every question -- so we
    pass them through with any_value() rather than aggregating.

    Granularity caveat
    ------------------
    sub_department is currently null on every input row (raw_engagement
    emits dept-level only in this build). The compound key here is
    therefore (survey_cycle, department, theme) in practice, with
    sub_department reserved for forward compatibility when the
    generator emits sub-dept rows for sub-depts with 5+ respondents.
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with engagement as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_engagement') }}
),

per_theme as (
    select
        survey_cycle,
        department,
        sub_department,
        theme,

        round(avg(avg_score),     2) as theme_avg_score,
        round(avg(favorable_pct), 4) as theme_avg_favorable_pct,
        count(*)                     as questions_in_theme,

        -- Denormalized in source -- same value across all question rows
        any_value(response_count) as response_count,
        any_value(enps_score)     as enps_score
    from engagement
    group by survey_cycle, department, sub_department, theme
),

with_cycle_date as (
    select
        *,
        case substr(survey_cycle, 6, 2)
            when 'Q1' then date(cast(left(survey_cycle, 4) as int64),  3, 31)
            when 'Q2' then date(cast(left(survey_cycle, 4) as int64),  6, 30)
            when 'Q3' then date(cast(left(survey_cycle, 4) as int64),  9, 30)
            when 'Q4' then date(cast(left(survey_cycle, 4) as int64), 12, 31)
        end as cycle_end_date
    from per_theme
),

with_trends as (
    select
        *,
        lag(theme_avg_score) over (
            partition by department, sub_department, theme
            order by cycle_end_date
        ) as prior_theme_avg_score,
        lag(theme_avg_favorable_pct) over (
            partition by department, sub_department, theme
            order by cycle_end_date
        ) as prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct
    from with_cycle_date
),

final as (
    select
        survey_cycle,
        cycle_end_date,
        department,
        sub_department,
        theme,
        questions_in_theme,
        response_count,
        theme_avg_score,
        theme_avg_favorable_pct,
        enps_score,
        prior_theme_avg_score,
        prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct,
        case
            when prior_theme_avg_score is not null
                then round(theme_avg_score - prior_theme_avg_score, 2)
            else null
        end as theme_score_delta,
        case
            when prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct is not null
                then round(theme_avg_favorable_pct - prior_theme_avg_favorable_pct, 4)
            else null
        end as theme_favorable_pct_delta
    from with_trends
)

select * from final

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.name: int_employee_tenure
model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.description:
Per-employee tenure and career-velocity calculations. One row per
employee. Reference date for "current" is var('current_date'),
defaulting to 2025-03-31 (the synthetic dataset's current
quarter); for terminated employees the reference date is their
termination_date so tenure stops at departure.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description: FK to stg_employees. One row per employee. Primary key.

termination_date.name: termination_date
termination_date.description: Carried forward from stg_employees. Null for active employees.

reference_date.name: reference_date
reference_date.description:
The date used as "today" in all tenure calculations.
COALESCE(termination_date, var('current_date')). Always
populated; always >= hire_date.

tenure_months.name: tenure_months
tenure_months.description:
Whole months between hire_date and reference_date. Always
non-negative. Active employees with same-day hires return 0.

tenure_years.name: tenure_years
tenure_years.description:
Tenure in years (days / 365.25, rounded to 2 decimals). Always
non-negative.

total_promotions.name: total_promotions
total_promotions.description:
Count of Promotion events in stg_job_history for the employee.
Includes IC->IC steps and IC->M jumps but NOT Title Change
rows (which encode the Manager Step-Back's M1->IC4 demotion).
Zero for employees who have never been promoted.

last_promotion_date.name: last_promotion_date
last_promotion_date.description:
Effective date of the employee's most recent Promotion event.
Null for employees who have never been promoted.

months_since_last_promotion.name: months_since_last_promotion
months_since_last_promotion.description:
Whole months from last_promotion_date to reference_date.
Null for employees who have never been promoted.

time_in_current_role_months.name: time_in_current_role_months
time_in_current_role_months.description:
Whole months from the most recent event of any change_type
(Hire / Promotion / Lateral Transfer / Title Change /
Manager Change) to reference_date. Approximates time spent
in the current role configuration.

last_manager_change_date.name: last_manager_change_date
last_manager_change_date.description:
Effective date of the employee's most recent Manager Change
event. Null for employees who have never had a manager change
(most employees -- the original at-hire manager remains
unchanged in raw_job_history when at-hire == current).

has_manager_change_last_12mo.name: has_manager_change_last_12mo
has_manager_change_last_12mo.description:
TRUE when a Manager Change occurred within 12 months prior to
reference_date. FALSE otherwise. Never null -- employees with
no Manager Change history return FALSE, not NULL.

career_velocity_per_year.name: career_velocity_per_year
career_velocity_per_year.description:
Promotions per tenure-year. Computed as
  total_promotions * 365.25 / tenure_in_days
rounded to 3 decimals. Returns 0 for same-day hires (avoids
divide-by-zero). High values (> 0.5) indicate fast career
progression typical of High-flyer and Founder archetypes.

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        int_employee_tenure
    Layer:        intermediate
    Sources:      stg_employees, stg_job_history
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml intermediate defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    One row per employee with all tenure / career-velocity calculations
    that downstream marts need. Centralizing these here means the math
    is defined once and consumed many times -- changes to the spec
    formulas only touch this file.

    Reference date
    --------------
    All calculations anchor on a "snapshot date" rather than wall-clock
    today, so the synthetic dataset's analytics stay stable as the real
    date drifts forward. The default snapshot is var('current_date')
    (2025-03-31, the spec's current quarter); for active employees the
    reference date used in calculations is the snapshot, for terminated
    employees it is their termination_date.

    Calculated fields
    -----------------
        tenure_months                   reference_date - hire_date in months
        tenure_years                    reference_date - hire_date in days / 365.25
        total_promotions                count of Promotion events
        last_promotion_date             max(effective_date where Promotion); null if never promoted
        months_since_last_promotion     reference_date - last_promotion_date; null if never promoted
        time_in_current_role_months     reference_date - max(effective_date across all change_types)
        last_manager_change_date        max(effective_date where Manager Change); null if no MC
        has_manager_change_last_12mo    bool: any MC within 12 months of reference_date
        career_velocity_per_year        total_promotions divided by tenure in years

    Edge cases
    ----------
    - Employees with no Promotion events: total_promotions = 0;
      last_promotion_date / months_since_last_promotion are NULL;
      career_velocity_per_year is 0.
    - Employees whose Hire date equals the reference date (tenure 0
      days): career_velocity_per_year is 0 (avoids divide-by-zero).
    - has_manager_change_last_12mo is false (not null) when no Manager
      Change has ever occurred for the employee.
*/

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

with employees as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_employees') }}
),

job_history as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_job_history') }}
),

snapshot as (
    select cast('{{ var("current_date") }}' as date) as snapshot_date
),

-- Reference date per employee: termination_date if terminated, else snapshot.
employee_reference_date as (
    select
        employees.employee_id,
        employees.hire_date,
        employees.termination_date,
        coalesce(employees.termination_date, snapshot.snapshot_date) as reference_date
    from employees
    cross join snapshot
),

-- Promotion stats per employee.
promotion_stats as (
    select
        employee_id,
        count(*)                  as total_promotions,
        max(effective_date)       as last_promotion_date
    from job_history
    where change_type = 'Promotion'
    group by employee_id
),

-- Most recent Manager Change per employee.
manager_change_recency as (
    select
        employee_id,
        max(effective_date) as last_manager_change_date
    from job_history
    where change_type = 'Manager Change'
    group by employee_id
),

-- Most recent event of any change_type per employee. The Hire row
-- guarantees this is non-null for every employee.
last_event as (
    select
        employee_id,
        max(effective_date) as last_event_date
    from job_history
    group by employee_id
),

final as (
    select
        e.employee_id,

        -- Anchor dates
        e.hire_date,
        e.termination_date,
        e.reference_date,

        -- Tenure
        date_diff(e.reference_date, e.hire_date, month)            as tenure_months,
        round(date_diff(e.reference_date, e.hire_date, day) / 365.25, 2) as tenure_years,

        -- Promotion stats
        coalesce(ps.total_promotions, 0) as total_promotions,
        ps.last_promotion_date,
        case
            when ps.last_promotion_date is not null
                then date_diff(e.reference_date, ps.last_promotion_date, month)
            else null
        end as months_since_last_promotion,

        -- Time in current role (from most recent event of any kind).
        -- COALESCE on hire_date is defensive; the Hire event guarantees
        -- last_event_date is always populated.
        date_diff(
            e.reference_date,
            coalesce(le.last_event_date, e.hire_date),
            month
        ) as time_in_current_role_months,

        -- Manager change recency
        mcr.last_manager_change_date,
        case
            when mcr.last_manager_change_date is not null
                and date_diff(e.reference_date, mcr.last_manager_change_date, month) <= 12
                then true
            else false
        end as has_manager_change_last_12mo,

        -- Career velocity (promotions per tenure-year). Avoid
        -- divide-by-zero for same-day hires.
        case
            when date_diff(e.reference_date, e.hire_date, day) > 0
                then round(
                    coalesce(ps.total_promotions, 0)
                    * 365.25
                    / date_diff(e.reference_date, e.hire_date, day),
                    3
                )
            else 0
        end as career_velocity_per_year
    from employee_reference_date as e
    left join promotion_stats        as ps  on e.employee_id = ps.employee_id
    left join manager_change_recency as mcr on e.employee_id = mcr.employee_id
    left join last_event             as le  on e.employee_id = le.employee_id
)

select * from final

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.name: int_employee_performance_history
model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.description:
Per-cycle performance history with rating_numeric, is_top_performer
(2-consecutive-cycle definition per data dict), and trend signals
(cycle_sequence, prior_overall_rating, overall_rating_delta,
self_optimism_delta). One row per (employee_id, review_cycle).
Marts that only need the latest row per employee should filter on
cycle_sequence equal to the per-employee max.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.description:
FK to stg_employees. Not every employee appears here -- 2025-Q1
hires and very-short-tenure Early Churners have no eligible
review cycles.

review_cycle.name: review_cycle
review_cycle.description:
Cycle identifier in YYYY-HN format. Carried forward from
stg_performance.

cycle_end_date.name: cycle_end_date
cycle_end_date.description:
Calendar date the cycle's review window closed. Parsed from
review_cycle. H1 -> July 15 of YYYY; H2 -> January 15 of YYYY+1.
Used as the ordering key for cycle_sequence and lag windows.

cycle_sequence.name: cycle_sequence
cycle_sequence.description:
1-based ordinal of this cycle within the employee's review
history. cycle_sequence = 1 is the first eligible cycle for
the employee.

review_status.name: review_status
review_status.description:
Carried forward from stg_performance. Calculated fields apply
uniformly across status; marts that exclude Exempt or
Incomplete should filter at consumption time.

overall_rating_numeric.name: overall_rating_numeric
overall_rating_numeric.description:
Numeric mapping of overall_rating per data dict
(SE=5 / E=4 / M=3 / PM=2 / DNM=1). Used for arithmetic
aggregation downstream (averages, deltas, trends).

manager_rating_numeric.name: manager_rating_numeric
manager_rating_numeric.description: Numeric mapping of manager_rating (same scale as overall).

self_rating.name: self_rating
self_rating.description: Carried forward from stg_performance. Nullable per spec.

self_rating_numeric.name: self_rating_numeric
self_rating_numeric.description:
Numeric mapping of self_rating. Null when self_rating is
null.

prior_overall_rating.name: prior_overall_rating
prior_overall_rating.description:
The overall_rating from the immediately previous cycle for
this employee, ordered by cycle_end_date. Null on
cycle_sequence = 1.

prior_overall_rating_numeric.name: prior_overall_rating_numeric
prior_overall_rating_numeric.description:
Numeric mapping of prior_overall_rating. Null on
cycle_sequence = 1.

overall_rating_delta.name: overall_rating_delta
overall_rating_delta.description:
overall_rating_numeric minus prior_overall_rating_numeric.
Range [-4, +4]. Null on cycle_sequence = 1. Positive =
improving; negative = declining (the canonical signal for
Performance Managed Out trajectories).

is_top_performer.name: is_top_performer
is_top_performer.description:
Spec calculated field: TRUE when overall_rating IN
('Significantly Exceeds', 'Exceeds') for this cycle AND the
prior cycle (2 consecutive cycles). Always FALSE on
cycle_sequence = 1 (no prior cycle to compare).

self_optimism_delta.name: self_optimism_delta
self_optimism_delta.description:
self_rating_numeric minus overall_rating_numeric. Range
[-4, +4]. Positive = self-rating exceeds calibrated overall
(typical optimism bias, ~30% of reviews). Null when
self_rating is null.

model.justkaizen_analytics.int_employee_.raw_code:
/*
    Model:        int_employee_performance_history
    Layer:        intermediate
    Sources:      stg_performance
    Materialized: view  (per dbt_project.yml intermediate defaults)

    Purpose
    -------
    Per-cycle performance history with the spec's two calculated fields
    plus auxiliary trend signals downstream consumers will need. One row
    per (employee_id, review_cycle), preserving the full history. Marts
    that only need the latest row per employee can filter on
    cycle_sequence = max(cycle_sequence) over (...) downstream.

    Calculated fields
    -----------------
        rating_numeric (per rating column)
            SE=5, E=4, M=3, PM=2, DNM=1 per data dict. Computed for
            overall_rating, manager_rating, and self_rating
            independently. Self_rating_numeric is null when self_rating
            is null.

        cycle_end_date
            Parsed from review_cycle (e.g. '2024-H2' -> 2025-01-15).
            H1 cycles complete July 15 of the same year; H2 cycles
            complete January 15 of the following year (per Section 7).

        cycle_sequence
